footer.tagline: Web Development · Dog Training · Game Development

main.lead1: I design websites, study the relationship between people and dogs, and experiment with game development.
main.lead2: Three different paths, one direction: observe, simplify and build something that truly works.

manifesto.title: Three directions. One way of working.
manifesto.intro: I'm not interested in adding complexity where it isn't needed. In every project I start with the essentials: understanding the context, observing what matters, and building an experience that's simpler to live with.

clarity.context: On the web I look for
clarity.desc: Understand the context, remove the superfluous and make every page easy to read.

comprehension.desc: Observe the individual, listen to their signals and build trust, one step at a time.

interaction.context: In video games I look for
interaction.desc: Turn an idea into a mechanic, and a mechanic into an experience worth playing.

web.title: Websites that tell who you really are.
web.intro: For over 8 years I've been designing and developing showcase websites for professionals, local businesses and companies. I create digital experiences that are tidy, fast and built to help visitors immediately grasp one thing: why choose you.
web.mockupAltDesktop: Desktop mockup of a site developed by LeMaks
web.mockupAltMobile: Mobile mockup of a site developed by LeMaks
web.block1Title: I design sites that work before they look good.
web.block1Muted: An effective site doesn't just need good design. It must guide, explain, reassure and lead people toward a concrete action.

showcase.title: Showcase sites
showcase.desc: For professionals and businesses who want to tell their story clearly.

corporate.title: Corporate sites
corporate.desc: For companies that need a credible and well-organised online presence.

landing.title: Landing pages
landing.desc: For services, campaigns, products or ideas to be presented directly.

restyling.title: Restyling
restyling.desc: To turn a dated site into a clearer, faster and more up-to-date tool.

web.block2Title: Strategy, design and development in the same path.
web.block2Muted: Every project starts with a simple question: what should a person understand when they land on your site? From there I build structure, content, design and development.

process.05: Publish a site ready to grow

game.title: I build games for the web. Now I'm also exploring Godot.
game.intro1: I've already created games as web apps, turning ideas and mechanics into experiences accessible right from the browser.
game.intro2: Today I'm digging deeper into Godot to approach game development in a more structured way: prototyping, game design, physics, interactions and building small playable worlds.

browserBlock.title: From the browser to the game engine.
browserBlock.p1: My journey in game development started on the web: games and small interactive experiences built to be immediate, accessible and playable anywhere.
browserBlock.p2: Now I'm studying Godot to broaden the way I design games, experimenting with tools designed specifically for game development.
browserBlock.p3: I'm not starting from scratch: I bring the experience I built in web development, interface design and creating clear interactions.

webApp.title: Web app games
webApp.desc: Interactive experiences accessible from the browser, designed to be easy to launch and engaging to play.

prototypes.title: Prototypes and mechanics
prototypes.desc: Small experiments to test ideas, rules, pacing and game feedback.

godot.title: Godot projects
godot.desc: New exercises and prototypes built to deepen my 2D development, GDScript, physics and level design.

spotlight.title: A word game, designed for the screen in your hand.
spotlight.p1: A small web game inspired by guess-the-word games: simple to understand, quick to play and designed to work best on a smartphone.
spotlight.p2: The goal is to guess the right word through attempts, hints and visual feedback. Every choice must be immediate, readable and satisfying.
spotlight.note: The game is mainly optimised for mobile devices. The screens shown reflect the smartphone experience.

projects.title: Ideas that become playable.
projects.muted1: Here I gather web games, prototypes and projects in development.
projects.muted2: Some are born to explore a mechanic, others to create a complete experience. Each project is a chance to better understand how interaction, pacing, difficulty and fun really work.

proto.imageAlt: Visual concept of the first web game
proto.title: First Godot game
proto.desc: An interactive experience built for the browser, between simple mechanics and game pacing.
proto.aiNote: Visual concept generated with AI, used as a placeholder to convey the idea and atmosphere of the game. Not final artwork.

vision.title: Building experiences that invite you to play.
vision.p1: I want to keep building games for the web and, at the same time, grow my skills with Godot.
vision.p2: The goal is to combine what I already know about the web with tools and logic specific to game development, to create more curated, interactive and memorable experiences.

cinofila.title: Understanding the dog is the first step in training them.
cinofila.intro: I'm studying the cognitive-relational method to learn to observe the dog as an individual: with emotions, needs, experiences and their own way of communicating. A learning journey carried out with seriousness, study and respect.
cinofila.photoAlt3: A moment of connection with the dog

approach.title: Every dog has a story. That's where we begin.
approach.p1Before: There are no standard solutions that work for everyone. Every path starts from
approach.p1After: of the dog, the context they live in and the needs of their family.
approach.p2After: . The goal isn't a perfect dog, but a dog who is calmer, listened to and able to face the world alongside their person.

topics.title: Small steps, useful in real life.

puppies.title: Puppies and first months
puppies.desc: Routines, settling into the family and building the first foundations.
puppies.hint: Building calm habits right from the start.

communication.title: Communication
communication.desc: Learning to read signals, needs and moments of difficulty.

walking.title: Walking and environment
walking.desc: Creating calmer experiences inside and outside the home.
walking.hint: The environment is part of the education.

adopted.title: Adopted dogs
adopted.desc: Welcoming the dog's history and gradually building new habits.

diary.title: I study, observe, learn.
diary.desc: This space gathers notes, reflections and observations from my learning journey. It doesn't aim to give absolute answers: it wants to share better questions and a more mindful way of being alongside dogs.

articles.title: I write about what I'm building.
articles.p1: Here I collect articles, technical notes and deep dives born from my projects.
articles.p2: It's not auto-published content: I only pick the ones that best represent how I work, the technologies I use and the ideas I want to share.

featured.title: Featured article
featured.articleTitle: Enhancing React Development with Dynamic Styled Components and a Custom Grid System
featured.articleDesc: A deep dive into how to use custom functions and utilities with styled-components to manage colours, spacing, breakpoints and a tailor-made responsive grid system.

about.title: I design, observe, experiment.
about.p1: I'm Maksim, a web developer with over 8 years of experience. The web is my main job: I design showcase sites to help businesses, professionals and companies tell their story better online.
about.p2: Alongside work, I'm exploring two paths that are teaching me a lot: cognitive-relational dog training and game development with Godot.
about.p3: They're different fields, but they have something in common: they require listening, observation, patience and the ability to build experiences that make sense for those who live them.

services.title: What I can do for you
services.subtitle: I work with local businesses, associations, events and small operations. Drop me a line to see if I can help.

local.title: Sites for events and local businesses
local.desc: Fast, mobile and easy-to-share landing pages: schedule, maps, contacts, sign-ups and social links. All in one place, so information doesn't stay scattered between posts and flyers.

automation.title: Automations to reduce manual work
automation.desc: Forms, requests, confirmations, reminders, management sheets and small processes wired together. Fewer messages to handle by hand, more time for the work that matters.

services.cta: Let's talk about your site or your automation →

contact.title: Let's talk about your site or your automation
contact.subtitle: Do you have an event, a business or a process you want to make easier to run? Drop me a line: I reply within 24 hours to figure out together if I can help.
contact.privacyNote: The data entered will be used exclusively to reply to your contact request and will not be used for marketing purposes without your consent.

s1.title: 1. Data Controller
s1.p1: The data controller is Maksim Leali, reachable by email:

s2.title: 2. Personal data processed
s2.sub1: Data provided via the contact form
s2.p1: Through the contact form on the site, the following data may be processed:
s2.l3: company or project, if provided by the user;
s2.l4: details about the request or service of interest;
s2.l6: any additional data the user voluntarily chooses to share.
s2.p2: Users are advised not to submit unnecessary personal data, special categories of personal data, or confidential information through the form.
s2.p3: The site uses Umami, a self-hosted analytics system managed by the Controller via analytics.lemaks.it.
s2.p4: Umami is used solely to understand, in aggregated and statistical form, how the site is used. Technical and navigation data may be processed, including:
s2.l7: page visited, page title and referrer domain;
s2.l8: browser type, operating system, device type, language and screen resolution;
s2.p5: The tracker is configured not to collect search parameters or URL fragments. The IP address may be temporarily used by the system to derive geographic statistics, but is not stored by Umami.
s2.p6: The site does not use profiling cookies, advertising cookies, remarketing tools, or technologies designed to track the user across different sites.
s2.p7: For security, maintenance and abuse-prevention purposes, the server may log technical data such as IP address, date and time of request, requested resource, response code and browser information.

s3.title: 3. Purposes of processing
s3.l1: respond to requests submitted via the contact form;
s3.l2: follow up with the user regarding the received request;
s3.l3: handle information requests, collaborations or quotes;
s3.l4: ensure security, maintenance and proper functioning of the site;
s3.l5: produce aggregated statistics on site usage and improve its content, structure and performance;
s3.l6: protect the Controller's rights in case of abuse, violations or disputes.
s3.p2: Data are not used for newsletters, commercial communications, profiling or direct marketing without the user's specific consent.

s4.title: 4. Legal basis of processing
s4.l1: for the execution of pre-contractual measures taken at the request of the data subject, when the user requests information, contact or a quote;
s4.l2: for the legitimate interest of the Controller in ensuring site security, preventing abuse and analysing its usage in aggregate form;
s4.l3: based on the user's consent, exclusively when required for additional non-necessary purposes, such as any future marketing communications.

s5.title: 5. Data provision
s5.p1: Providing the data marked as mandatory in the contact form is necessary to submit and manage the request.
s5.p2: Failure to provide them may make it impossible to respond to the user.

s6.title: 6. Processing methods
s6.p1: Data submitted via the contact form are not stored in a site database. They are transmitted by email to the Controller's contact inbox and used solely to handle the user's request.
s6.p2: Navigation statistics are stored in a separate database used exclusively by the self-hosted Umami system.
s6.p3: Processing is carried out through IT and electronic tools, adopting appropriate technical and organisational measures to prevent unauthorised access, loss, alteration or disclosure of data.

s7.title: 7. Recipients of data
s7.p1: Personal data may be processed by the following parties:
s7.l2: DigitalOcean, LLC, as cloud infrastructure and hosting provider for the site and the self-hosted analytics system;
s7.l3: provider of the email service and/or message delivery generated by the contact form;
s7.l4: any parties authorised by the Controller or technical providers strictly necessary for site management, security and maintenance.
s7.p2: Data are not shared, sold or communicated to third parties for advertising or marketing purposes.

s8.title: 8. Transfers outside the European Economic Area
s8.p1: The site and the Umami analytics system are hosted on DigitalOcean infrastructure located within the European Economic Area.
s8.p2: Should a transfer outside the European Economic Area become necessary for technical reasons related to the services provided by the listed suppliers, such transfer will comply with the safeguards set out in Articles 44 and following of EU Regulation 2016/679.
s8.p3: Information on the applicable safeguards can be requested by contacting the Controller.

s9.title: 9. Retention period
s9.p1Pre: Messages received via the contact form are retained for the time necessary to handle the request and, in any case, no longer than
s9.p1Post: from receipt, unless further retention is required by law or to protect the Controller's rights.
s9.p2Pre: Statistical data collected via Umami are retained for a maximum period of
s9.p3Pre: Server technical logs are retained for the time strictly necessary for security and maintenance purposes, and generally no longer than
s9.p3Post: unless further retention is required due to security incidents or requests from competent authorities.

s10.title: 10. Rights of the data subject
s10.p1: Users may exercise the rights provided by Articles 15-22 of EU Regulation 2016/679, including:
s10.l1: obtain confirmation of the existence of their personal data;
s10.l2: request access, rectification or erasure of data;
s10.l6: withdraw consent at any time, where processing is based on consent.
s10.p2: To exercise their rights, users may contact the Controller at:
s10.p3: Users also have the right to lodge a complaint with the relevant supervisory authority.

s11.title: 11. Automated decisions and profiling
s11.p1: Personal data are not used for automated decision-making processes or profiling activities.

s12.title: 12. Changes to this policy
s12.p1: This policy may be updated over time. Any changes will be published on this page with the date of the latest update.

s1.title: 1. What are cookies
s1.p1: Cookies are small text files that websites can save on the user's device to enable certain features, store preferences or analyse navigation.
s1.p2: Some analytics technologies may collect information without using cookies.

s2.title: 2. Cookies used by this site
s2.p1: The site lemaks.it does not use profiling cookies, advertising cookies, remarketing cookies or cookies intended to track users across different websites.
s2.p2: The site does not use third-party analytics cookies such as Google Analytics, Meta Pixel, Microsoft Clarity, Hotjar or equivalent tools.

s3.title: 3. Self-hosted analytics
s3.p1: The site uses Umami, a self-hosted analytics system accessible via analytics.lemaks.it.
s3.p2: Umami does not use cookies and does not store IP addresses. The service is used exclusively to produce aggregated statistics on site traffic, such as pages visited, visit source, browser, device, language and approximate geographic area.
s3.l1: limit operation to the lemaks.it and www.lemaks.it domains;
s3.l2: not collect search parameters or URL fragments;
s3.l3: respect the browser's "Do Not Track" setting;
s3.l4: not send data contained in contact forms;
s3.l5: not use personal identifiers, profiling, behavioural advertising or cross-site tracking.
s3.p4: No session replay tools, heatmaps, conversion pixels, persistent identifiers or equivalent features are active.

s4.title: 4. Similar technologies: local language storage
s4.p1Pre: The site is available in multiple languages (Italian, English, French and Spanish). To remember the user's chosen language between visits, the site uses the
s4.p2: This is not a cookie: the value is never sent to the server, contains no personal data, is not shared with third parties and cannot be used to profile the user or track them across other sites.
s4.p3Post: to provide the language-switching feature explicitly requested by the user, and therefore does not require prior consent.
s4.p4: Users can delete the preference at any time from the browser settings ("site data" / "storage" / "cookies and site data" section).

s5.title: 5. Consent and cookie banner
s5.p1: Since the site does not use cookies or tracking tools for profiling or marketing purposes, and the technologies used (aggregated statistics and language storage) are strictly necessary for the site to function, prior consent via a cookie banner is not requested.
s5.p2: Users can nonetheless prevent statistics from being sent by enabling the "Do Not Track" setting in their browser.

s6.title: 6. Data processed and retention period
s6.p1: Information collected via Umami is used exclusively to analyse and improve the site.
s6.p2Pre: Statistical data are retained for a maximum period of
s6.p3: The language preference stored locally in the browser persists until the user manually deletes it or clears site data from the browser settings.
s6.p4Pre: For further information on personal data processing and user rights, please see the

s7.title: 7. Changes to the Cookie Policy
s7.p1: This Cookie Policy may be updated over time. Any changes will be published on this page with the date of the latest update.

s2.p2: The information published is of a general nature and does not constitute a quote, binding contractual offer, professional advice, promise of result or guarantee of availability of the described services.

s4.p2: Copying, reproducing, modifying, distributing, publishing or using such content for commercial purposes without prior written authorisation from the Controller is not permitted, except as allowed by law.

s5.p2: The Controller does not control such resources and is not responsible for their content, availability, security, privacy policy, cookie policy or terms of use. Users are invited to review the applicable policies and conditions of external services before using them.

s6.title: 6. Availability and limitation of liability

s6.p2: To the extent permitted by law, the Controller is not liable for direct or indirect damages arising from the use or inability to use the site, its content or linked external services.
